name: India
summary: Day 105 / 2026-06-12. **THE WAR FINALLY HELPED INDIA — A DEAL+OIL RELIEF RALLY (NIFTY +2.0%, SENSEX +2.3%, RUPEE FIRMER) AS THE DE-ESCALATION/OIL-DOWN CHANNEL FIRED POSITIVE FOR THE FIRST TIME.** US-Iran deal hopes plus Brent -4% eased India's import-cost, fiscal and inflation pressure; financials led (Bank Nifty +3.0%) and the rupee firmed to ₹95.10, AWAY from the ₹96 line that was un-ticking yesterday. The Indian press swung from the Settebello sovereignty-grievance back to the upside within a day. The Settebello fallout advanced only procedurally (demarche to US Charge Jason Meeks; 'deepest concern over the ongoing incidents'); with the blockade staying until a deal signs, the irritant persists short-term but the deal track caps the rupture risk. Deployment scorecard, IMPROVING for the first time: Brent <$95 [✓ $86.71], organic rupee toward ₹93-94 [closer, ₹95.10], FII net-positive 3+ sessions [STILL UNCONFIRMED]. The conditions are improving — but on a ~80-85% deal HOPE, not a signature, and not yet an FII-flow turn. No deployment signal yet.

current.price: Nifty 24,085 / Sensex 77,155 (17 Jun close); USD/INR Rs94.51; India 10Y ~6.86%; Brent $78.35.
current.delta_1d: Held 24,000 (4th up day) on importer dividend + DII; pre-Fed. Thursday faces hawkish-Fed/dollar headwind.
current.rupee: USD/INR ~95.40 (10 Jun, -0.3% 1D) — fell 77p to close 95.71 Mon 8 Jun on escalation, then firmed to 95.47 (Tue) / ~95.40 (Wed) as the market shifted to trading Trump's tone. Rs94.50 DECISIVELY BROKEN; Rs96 pressed but NOT breached onshore. RBI's June 5 MPC package credited with stabilisation off the 96-97 record-low zone.
current.fii: FII STILL NET-SELLING both observed sessions: net -Rs5,556cr Mon 8 Jun, ~-Rs4,566cr Tue 9 Jun. DII net +Rs5,165cr Mon absorbed ~93% of the FII selling — the DII buffer remains THE transmission mechanism keeping Nifty rangebound and held the 23,000 floor. The deployment sufficient condition (FII net-positive 3+ consecutive sessions) remains UNMET. YTD CY26 outflow ~Rs2.5-3.0 lakh crore (exceeds 2025 full-year record); foreign-investor bet at a decade low, below DII for the first time.
current.policy: RBI HELD repo at 5.25% (3rd straight hold, neutral, hike-bias). The June 5 MPC macro-prudential package (FCNR(B) hedging-cost subsidies, concessional ECB swaps, FPI G-sec tax exemptions, expanded Fully Accessible Route) is credited with stabilising the rupee and easing the India 10Y to ~6.95-7.00% despite oil.
current.read: LIMITED-EXPOSURE RESILIENCE. Monday's gap-down test resolved BULLISHLY over three sessions — the imported US AI/rates risk-off was SHALLOW and the DII buffer held the 23,000 floor; the market then rose on Trump's 'peace talks' tone (Tue) and THROUGH an actual three-country missile strike (Wed), led by FMCG/private banks/healthcare on limited global-commodity-supply-chain exposure. The diaspora tell: NO India evacuation/MEA-advisory signals despite Iranian missiles landing in three Gulf/Levant states hosting Indian workers — the security establishment still reads this as calibrated theater. The deployment sufficient condition (FII net-positive 3+ sessions) is UNMET; the re-rating still needs an FII turn, not a ceasefire.

active_triggers.condition: Rupee crosses ₹95/USD — long since re-breached and well beyond. Current ~₹95.40 (10 Jun; ₹95.71 Mon 8 Jun close). WATCHING.
active_triggers.note_2026-06-10: 2026-06-10: CORRECTED a badly stale '₹92.55' current-level reference — the rupee is at ~₹95.40 (closed ₹95.71 Mon 8 Jun on escalation, firmed since on Trump's tone). The ₹95 level is long since re-breached; ₹94.50 decisively broken; ₹96 pressed but not breached onshore. Stays WATCHING (the meaningful live level is the ₹96 RBI-direct-intervention trigger on inr-usd).

active_triggers.note_2026-06-16: 2026-06-16: Brent $82.92 (one session sub-$85) but Tuesday reversing on Lebanon — not yet sustained. STAYS watching.
active_triggers.note_2026-06-17: 2026-06-17: Now sustained sub-$85 for 2 sessions (Brent $78.65) as blockade lifts -- the import-cost relief is materialising. The India-positive condition is firming.

active_triggers.condition: India announces fuel-price pass-through OR Iran-rupee oil track restart via Chabahar within 30 days (by 2026-05-27)
active_triggers.activation_note: Petrol/diesel raised ~90 paise on May 19 — SECOND hike in a week (first on May 12). The pass-through-as-policy choice is operationally fired inside the 30-day window (window opened 2026-04-27, closes 2026-05-27).
active_triggers.update_2026-05-25: Now firing HARDER — FOURTH hike May 25 (+₹2.61-2.71/L, the LARGEST single hike of the sequence), breaks the Saturday-only political-tolerance pattern (big Monday hike on a US/UK holiday). Cumulative ~₹7.5/L since May 15. Reads as cost-recovery/refining-margin + weak-rupee pass-through, not a political cadence. Stays `active_fired`.

top_edges.context: 2026-06-10: rupee fell 77p to close 95.71 Mon 8 Jun ('West Asia conflict escalates'), then firmed to 95.47 (Tue) / ~95.40 (Wed, -0.3%) as the tape shifted to Trump's tone. Rs94.50 decisively broken; Rs96 pressed but NOT breached onshore; RBI's June 5 MPC package credited with stabilisation off the 96-97 lows. Weight held at 10 (capped, directness-1).

2026-06-10: RBI's June 5 MPC macro-prudential package (FCNR(B) hedging-cost subsidies, concessional ECB swap windows, FPI G-sec tax exemptions, expanded Fully Accessible Route) is credited with stabilising the rupee off the 96-97 record-low zone and easing the India 10Y to ~6.95-7.00% despite oil — the regulatory toolbox working BEFORE it shows in prices. Weight held at 10.

2026-06-10: Monday's GIFT-indicated gap-down resolved bullishly over three sessions — Nifty 23,123 (-1.04%) Mon held above the indicated gap and the 23,000 floor on a ~93% DII absorption, then +0.52% Tue (Trump 'peace talks' remark) and +0.67% Wed midday THROUGH an overnight IRGC three-country strike (FMCG/private banks/healthcare led on limited commodity-supply-chain exposure). The market is trading Trump's tone not Iranian missiles. Weight held at 10 (capped, directness-1, dominant market-impact channel).

2026-06-04 (intel A12): refined — the 12.5% duty is a 54-country Section 301 action (China ALSO 12.5%, India NOT singled out), a PROPOSAL with comments due 22 Jun / hearings 7 Jul / decision late-Jun-Jul, and India is disputing it (GTRI) and negotiating it into the interim trade pact. Weight bumped modestly 4.0->4.5 (refined understanding, NOT a newly-levied duty); kept conservative because it is contingent and contestable — formula would give 6.0 but this is a proposal India is actively negotiating away.

recent_signals.headline: India held 24,000 on the importer dividend + DII buying, but the hawkish Fed (DXY>100) is a Thursday headwind and FII keep selling (Rs47,000cr June). The deployment case: macro tailwind real (oil sub-$80, rupee firm) but the two conviction triggers (published signed text Fri + FII turn) remain open, and the Fed just made the FII turn harder. CONFIRMED/REPORTED.

India's macro tailwind is fully in train -- oil sub-$80, rupee firm, 10Y eased to 6.863% -- and Nifty reclaimed 24,000. But the equity-flow confirmation (FII turn) is still absent and N-Asia AI is an active capital competitor. Posture: staged-and-ready; conviction triggers (published signed text Fri + FII turn) remain open. CONFIRMED/REPORTED.

India got its best macro setup of the war on the framework — oil -5%, rupee firmer to Rs94.55, 10Y easing — but the equity response (+1%) badly lagged North Asia (+3-7%), and the FII-flow conviction trigger is unmet. Correct posture: staged-and-ready, not deployed; the two conviction triggers (published signed text on 19 Jun + an FII-flow turn) both remain open. CONFIRMED/REPORTED.

RELIEF RALLY + SOVEREIGNTY ANGER FADING. The deal+oil-down drove a broad India rally (Nifty +2.0%, Sensex +2.3%, rupee firmer to ₹95.10); Indian media pivoted from the 11 Jun 'India vs America' Settebello grievance back to the upside (cheaper oil helps the importer). The Settebello protest advanced procedurally (demarche to US Charge Jason Meeks, 'deepest concern,' three Indian-crewed ships hit in four days) but is now the secondary frame. Deployment conditions improving (Brent $86.71, rupee ₹95.10) but on deal HOPE, not a signature or an FII turn.
recent_signals.verification: CONFIRMED

recent_signals.headline: FIRST INDIAN WAR FATALITIES, AT US HANDS -- SOVEREIGNTY PIVOT. 3 Indian sailors (Aditya Sharma, Shivanand Chaurasiya, Patnala Suresh) killed on the disabled MT Settebello off Oman (US strike, 10 Jun; confirmed dead 11 Jun; 21 of 24 crew rescued). MEA summoned the US Charge d'Affaires, lodged a 'strong protest'; Jaiswal: 'These attacks must cease and end'; Sonowal ordered repatriation. Indian media pivoted markets->sovereignty (India vs America); Zee ran the summons prominently, Business Standard stayed transactional. Markets held: Nifty -0.23% (23,162), FII -Rs2,125cr Wed, rupee 95.79 (re-approaching Rs96). ~3 weeks to the Section 301 tariff comment deadline.
recent_signals.verification: CONFIRMED (deaths, summons, market data)

recent_signals.headline: **LIMITED-EXPOSURE RESILIENCE — INDIA ABSORBED MONDAY'S GAP-DOWN SHALLOWLY AND THEN ROSE THROUGH AN ACTUAL THREE-COUNTRY MISSILE STRIKE; THE DIASPORA SILENCE IS THE TELL.** Monday's test resolved bullishly over three sessions: Mon 8 Jun Nifty 23,123 (-1.04%) / Sensex 73,524 (-0.97%) — the imported US AI/rates risk-off was SHALLOW (cash held above the ~23,091 GIFT-indicated gap and well above the 23,000 floor), DII net +Rs5,165cr absorbing ~93% of FII net -Rs5,556cr, rupee -77p to 95.71 ('West Asia conflict escalates'). The tape then flipped to trading Trump's TONE: +0.52% Tue 9 Jun on Trump's 'peace talks are on' remark, then +0.67% Wed 10 Jun midday (Sensex +600 to ~74,518) THROUGH overnight IRGC strikes on US bases in three countries — FMCG/private banks/healthcare LEADING on 'limited exposure to global commodity supply chains.' **THE DIASPORA TELL**: NO India evacuation/MEA-advisory/Air-India-surge signals despite Iranian missiles landing in three Gulf/Levant states hosting Indian workers — that silence means India's security establishment still reads this as calibrated theater. Snapshot: Nifty 23,214.95 +0.4%, Sensex 73,983 +0.6%, Bank Nifty 55,100 +1.9%, USD/INR 95.40 -0.3%. All India triggers stay watching (no fuel/Chabahar restart, no oil-threshold crossing, no MEA escalation step).
sources: Business Standard ('West Asia conflict escalates'; Mon 8 Jun closes; rupee 95.71)
Intel 2026-06-10 A7, B1 (session-by-session; Trump 'peace talks are on'; IRGC three-country strike; no MEA escalation step)
Markets snapshot 2026-06-10 (Nifty 23,214.95 +0.4%, Sensex 73,983 +0.6%, Bank Nifty 55,100 +1.9%, USD/INR 95.40 -0.3%; FMCG/private banks/healthcare led on limited commodity-supply-chain exposure)
recent_signals.verification: Mon 8 Jun Nifty 23,123 (-1.04%) / Sensex 73,524 (-0.97%) + FII -Rs5,556cr / DII +Rs5,165cr + rupee 95.71 = CONFIRMED (Business Standard, NSE/NSDL flow); Tue 9 Jun +0.52% + Wed 10 Jun midday +0.67% through IRGC three-country strike = CONFIRMED (market data + intel); FMCG/private banks/healthcare leading on limited-commodity-exposure = CONFIRMED (markets snapshot); absence of India evacuation/MEA-advisory step = CONFIRMED ABSENCE (intel — security establishment reads calibrated theater). No trigger moved to active — no threshold crossing, no MEA escalation.

recent_signals.headline: **INDIA TO OPEN MONDAY IMPORTING THE US AI/RATES RISK-OFF — GIFT NIFTY -1.52% INDICATES A ~1.5% GAP-DOWN.** India's cash market closed Friday (23,366.70) before Wall Street's Friday AI rout, so Monday 8 June is its first reaction; the futures lean toward the 'dragged-down' read over the bullish FII-rotation thesis. The crisis is a marginal driver (~10-15%); the dominant force is imported US macro (Fed higher-for-longer + AI re-rating). FII ownership is at a two-decade low below DII for the first time, with DIIs absorbing ~90% of outflows — India's proximate floor. (CONFIRMED futures datum; forward indicator of the open) (intel A4)
recent_signals.verification: CONFIRMED (futures/flow data; indicates Monday's open)

recent_signals.headline: **THE BINDING CONSTRAINT CRACKED HARD — BUT WHETHER INDIA IS THE BENEFICIARY IS THE OPEN, CONTESTED QUESTION.** The 5-June US session de-risked the crowded AI/semi/rates trade violently: Broadcom's AI-guidance miss + a hot May jobs report (172K vs ~85K) drove Nasdaq -4%, S&P -2.64%, SMH -9.2%, VIX +39.7% to ~21.5, gold -3.1% — a RATES/AI unwind, NOT a war risk-off, and the exact catalyst the India thesis named ('the FII turn needs the AI trade to crack'). **The India-rotation implication is two-camp ANALYST OPINION**: Kotak Institutional Equities — FPI flows STAY MUTED (India has limited AI-supply-chain exposure, didn't ride the capex up, faces its own growth/inflation/valuation drags, 'low attractiveness vs other EMs' — needs an idiosyncratic catalyst); Morgan Stanley GIC — rotate AI 'builders' -> 'adopters', add rest-of-world / EM equities. SYNTHESIS: necessary condition (AI crack) partially met; sufficient condition (capital choosing India) NOT demonstrated. India closed BEFORE the rout: Nifty 23,366.70 (-0.21%, -49.85 pts) / Sensex 74,243.34 (-0.2%) / Bank Nifty 54,496.25 (+0.3%) — an Asia-session print, NOT a verdict on the US crash; Monday 8 June is the real test. In the same session India was the relative-EM OUTPERFORMER (Nifty -0.2% vs KOSPI -5.5% / Taiwan -1.3% / EEM -6.5%). RBI held 5.25% (3rd straight hold, neutral, hike-bias). FIIs have withdrawn ~Rs2.54 lakh crore YTD 2026, already exceeding the ~Rs2.4 lakh crore record sold in all of 2025 — India's foreign-investor bet has shrunk to a DECADE LOW as the AI trade drew capital away (Outlook Business); DIIs absorb ~90% of the selling. Operative onshore rupee ~Rs95.63 (a yfinance Rs94.95 'firmer' print is a SUSPECT artifact, NOT encoded). The 12.5% Section 301 tariff remains a proposal India is negotiating into the trade pact.
sources: Markets dossier 2026-06-06 (5-June US AI rout: Broadcom miss + May jobs 172K -> Nasdaq -4%, S&P -2.64%, SMH -9.2%, VIX +39.7% ~21.5, gold -3.1%; Nifty 23,366.70 -0.21% Asia close before the rout; relative-EM outperformer vs KOSPI -5.5%/EEM -6.5%; operative onshore rupee ~Rs95.63, yfinance Rs94.95 suspect)
Kotak Institutional Equities (FPI flows stay muted; India limited AI-supply-chain exposure; low attractiveness vs other EMs)
Morgan Stanley Global Investment Committee (rotate AI builders -> adopters; add rest-of-world / EM)
Outlook Business (FII YTD ~Rs2.54 lakh crore, exceeds full-2025 record ~Rs2.4 lakh crore; foreign-investor bet at decade low; DIIs absorb ~90%)
recent_signals.verification: 5-June US AI rout + May jobs 172K = CONFIRMED (market data + BLS); Nifty 23,366.70 -0.21% 5-June close + relative-EM outperformance = CONFIRMED (market data), explicitly flagged as an Asia-session print BEFORE the US rout (Monday 8 June is the real test); FII ~Rs2.54 lakh crore YTD exceeding the 2025 record + decade-low foreign bet + DII ~90% absorption = REPORTED (Outlook Business); RBI hold 5.25% = CONFIRMED; India-rotation implication = ANALYST OPINION, two camps labeled (Kotak muted vs Morgan Stanley rotate-to-EM) — NOT fact; operative onshore rupee ~Rs95.63 / yfinance Rs94.95 = SUSPECT artifact (not encoded as firming). No trigger moved to active — rupee pressing-not-breaking Rs96; capital_substitution WATCH trigger advanced on leg one only, stays watching.

recent_signals.headline: **FIRST MARGINALLY-CONSTRUCTIVE SESSION IN WEEKS: RBI HELD 5.25%, RUPEE HELD SUB-Rs96, IT-SHOCK RESOLVED, AND THE FII-DRAINING AI TRADE CRACKED.** (1) RBI held the repo at 5.25% (3rd straight hold, neutral stance, hike-bias by year-end; CPI 5.1% / core 7.4%) — India in the same higher-for-longer regime as the US, with a hike lean. The rupee held ~Rs95.40-95.65, pressing-not-breaking the Rs96 intervention trigger; no large-scale intervention announced. (2) The binding constraint — FII rotation into the North-Asia AI supercycle — took its FIRST valuation crack (KOSPI -5.3%, Samsung -7%, SK Hynix -9% on Broadcom's miss), the first session the capital_substitution flow could plausibly reverse (AI sell -> India bid). (3) The 12.5% Section 301 tariff refined: it is a 54-country action (China also 12.5%, India not singled out), a proposal with a July decision, and India is negotiating it into the trade pact — contingent and contestable. (4) The diaspora story deepened: Ambassador Tripathi met the injured and visited the Central Mortuary over the Kuwait-airport Indian fatality; missions region-wide on alert; GCC closed ranks rhetorically (Qatar reversed to condemnation) but only Kuwait expelled diplomats. India is now an FII-flow + tariff story (not oil-crisis); today the FII-flow piece moved India's way for the first time, though a violent AI crack would hit India too first.
sources: Intel 2026-06-04 A6 (RBI hold 5.25%), A12 (tariff 54-country refined), A3 (Kuwait consular response, Qatar condemnation, expulsion Kuwait-only)
Markets dossier 2026-06-04 C8 (India stabilisation; FII-flow + tariff story), C1/E1 (AI crack = first day substitution moved India's way)
Business Today/NewsX (RBI hold); FXStreet (rupee ~95.40); The Tribune/ANI (Ambassador mortuary visit); Business Standard/Business Today (tariff scope)
recent_signals.verification: RBI hold 5.25% + neutral + CPI/core projections = CONFIRMED (multiple Indian dailies); rupee sub-Rs96 = CONFIRMED (FXStreet/yfinance ~95.40-95.65); tariff 54-country/China-also/July-decision = CONFIRMED (Business Standard/Business Today); Ambassador consular response + Qatar condemnation + Kuwait-only expulsion = CONFIRMED (intel A3); AI-crack-as-first-FII-reversal = STRUCTURAL ANALYSIS (markets C1/E1), potential not confirmed. No trigger moved to active — rupee held sub-Rs96, no threshold crossed; constructive read flagged as marginal/potential.

recent_signals.headline: **MOST INDIA-ADVERSE SINGLE DAY OF THE CRISIS: FIRST CONFIRMED INDIAN FATALITY + RECORD SINGLE-DAY FII OUTFLOW + NEW US TARIFF.** The civilian killed in the 3 June Kuwait-airport drone strike was an Indian national (several other Indians injured); India's MEA condemned the attack, is assisting the family/injured, and put 'Indian missions and posts across the region on alert' — the war becoming a domestic Indian political/security event for the first time (not yet an evacuation posture). On markets: FIIs sold a record ₹8,362.92cr net on 2 June, taking 2026 cumulative net outflow to ~$26.8bn (record); the rupee fell 28 paise to ₹95.64, pressing the ₹96 RBI-intervention trigger, partly on a NEW USTR-proposed 12.5% Section 301 tariff on India. The 2 June close was Nifty 23,382.60 (−0.70%) / Sensex 74,267.34 (−0.68%), 4th straight down session, with broad weakness (HUL −2.83%, ITC −2.53%) and an 'IT stock shock' from the tariff. India takes the oil headwind + a tariff headwind with no de-escalation relief.
sources: India MEA statement (3 June) via Business Standard, The Tribune, IANS, NewsX — official — Indian national killed, missions on alert
Al Jazeera ('Iranian drone attack kills Indian citizen in Kuwait') — independent corroboration
The Week (3 June; FII ₹8,362.92cr, $26.8bn YTD record; Nifty 23,382.60 / Sensex 74,267.34); Investing.com / NewsX
Bloomberg / The Week (USTR 12.5% Section 301 tariff on India)
Intel dossier 2026-06-03 A7, B1; markets 2026-06-03 C4, E1
recent_signals.verification: First Indian fatality + MEA 'missions on alert' = CONFIRMED (MEA official + Al Jazeera); FII ₹8,362.92cr / $26.8bn YTD record + ₹95.64 rupee = CONFIRMED market data; 12.5% US tariff = CONFIRMED (2 sources, markets E1); INDEX-CAUTION: The Week's 23,382.60/74,267.34 print = the 2 June close (not a fresh 3 June close — 3 June EOD pending). No trigger to active — ₹96 RBI-intervention trigger imminent but not yet crossed.

recent_signals.headline: **INDIA'S WEAKNESS NOW EMPIRICALLY STRUCTURAL+MECHANICAL — FELL ON CRISIS RELIEF; RBI RESERVES -$7.5bn/wk.** Nifty -1.5%, Sensex -1.4%, worst May in six years, even as every crisis variable moved India's way. Proximate cause: MSCI rebalance ($800m-$1bn passive outflow) + F&O expiry + FII rotation to North-Asia AI. RBI forex reserves fell to $681.4bn (wk ending 22 May, -$7.5bn). Rough rice +19.6% MoM (export-restriction/food-security). A Hormuz deal removes a headwind India already wasn't trading on; the re-rating needs an FII turn.
recent_signals.verification: Nifty/Sensex closes + worst May in 6yr = CONFIRMED; RBI reserves -$7.5bn = CONFIRMED (official); MSCI = REPORTED. Crisis was a TAILWIND on 29 May and India still fell.

recent_signals.headline: **INDIA REFUSED THE OIL-RELIEF BOUNCE AGAIN — A SECOND STRAIGHT DOWN SESSION DESPITE SUB-$100 OIL; THE WORST-POSITIONED MAJOR MARKET FOR THIS CRISIS.** May 28 EOD was NOT yet published at run time — operative prints are the May 27 closes: **Sensex -0.19% to 75,867.80; Nifty 50 -0.03% to 23,907.15; rupee ~₹95.70 (USD/INR ₹95.68, -0.2% 1D, +1.1% MoM, +5.1% 3M vs ~₹87.9 pre-crisis).** Two consecutive down sessions even with operative Brent sub-$100 is itself the signal: oil relief is necessary but NOT sufficient against record FII outflows and a 15-year-low FPI ownership — India takes the bounce ONLY on a SIGNED deal, not a 'largely negotiated'/now-deadlocked one punctuated by strikes. **Drivers (Business Standard)**: lingering US-Iran tension + foreign-fund outflows. **Structural decomposition (markets dossier C5) — India is the worst-positioned major market for this crisis**: oil-import-dependent (~85% crude imported), FII-dependent, expensive (Nifty ~24x P/E vs 10yr ~22x avg, among the most expensive EM), and LOSING the capital-allocation contest to the AI-semis EM (India -9% YTD vs Korea +90%, Taiwan +42%). The weakness splits ~60% crisis component (reverses on a signed deal + oil retreat) / ~40% valuation-rotation (does NOT reverse — foreign money rotating OUT of India INTO the Korea/Taiwan AI trade). Nifty held ~23,900; watch 23,500 (relief-rally floor). **FII/FPI**: FY26 cumulative outflows ~₹3.33 lakh crore (Republic World May 28). No new MEA escalation advisory or evacuation step tied to the May 28 exchange; standing Gulf safety advisories remain. (NOTE: May 28 India EOD close was NOT published at run time — figures above are the May 27 closes; May 28 close pending. Operative Brent ~$99, NOT the yfinance roll artifact.)
sources: Markets dossier 2026-05-28 C5 (India worst-positioned major market; ~60% crisis / ~40% valuation-rotation; India -9% YTD vs Korea +90% / Taiwan +42%; Nifty ~24x P/E)
Intel dossier 2026-05-28 A5 (no new MEA escalation step; standing Gulf advisories)
Business Standard May 28 (India second down session on lingering US-Iran tension + foreign-fund outflows; May 27 closes Sensex 75,867.80 -0.19% / Nifty 23,907.15 -0.03%)
Republic World May 28 (FY26 FII cumulative outflows ~₹3.33 lakh crore)
recent_signals.verification: May 27 closes Sensex 75,867.80 (-0.19%) / Nifty 23,907.15 (-0.03%) / USD/INR ₹95.68 = CONFIRMED market data (Business Standard + markets dossier); May 28 India EOD close NOT published at run time — flagged pending, NOT invented; second-down-session-despite-sub-$100-oil + worst-positioned-major-market + ~60/40 crisis/valuation split = CONFIRMED analytical read (markets dossier C5); FY26 FII outflows ~₹3.33 lakh cr = REPORTED (Republic World May 28); India -9% YTD vs Korea +90% / Taiwan +42% = CONFIRMED relative-performance data; no new MEA escalation step = CONFIRMED (intel A5). No trigger moved to active — market node, no confirmed-action trigger today.

recent_signals.headline: **STRATEGIC-AUTONOMY QUESTION ANSWERED + A CONSTRUCTIVE RBI SWAP — BUT THE RELIEF BOUNCE DID NOT COME (Nifty gave back the May 25 rally, rupee back to ₹95.68).** Three CONFIRMED developments over the ~48h window (no brief May 26). (1) **Quad FMs, New Delhi, May 26** (US/Rubio, India/Jaishankar, Japan, Australia): joint statement voiced 'deep concern over the Iranian closure' of Hormuz, reiterated UNCLOS navigational rights, condemned attacks on commercial shipping, and 'opposed any future measures inconsistent with UNCLOS, including imposition of tolls.' India put its name to anti-toll language on SUBSTANCE — directly relevant to the deal's unresolved Hormuz-tolls clause — while Jaishankar kept the register multilateral/legalistic ('safe and unimpeded maritime commerce,' 'scrupulous observing of international law'), aligning without surrendering the non-aligned posture. The meeting also launched a Fiji port project + an Indo-Pacific maritime-surveillance initiative and condemned the Pahalgam terror attack. (2) **RBI $5B 3Y USD/INR buy-sell swap (May 26) drew ~1.96x cover** — 254 bids / $9.8bn, 141 accepted, ₹9.10 cut-off forward premium (near-leg settle May 29 2026, far-leg May 29 2029): HEALTHY bank dollar-appetite, NOT acute funding stress; pairs with the May 25 'rupee undervalued' jawboning = coordinated liquidity+verbal support. (3) **BUT the relief bounce did NOT arrive.** The US conducted CONFIRMED self-defense strikes inside southern Iran May 25 (IRGC mine-laying boats + a Bandar Abbas SAM site); Iran VOWED retaliation but has NOT acted (rhetoric only). The strikes 'dampened hopes for a peace deal' — on Tue May 26 (US desks reopened) India gave back Monday's rally: **Nifty 50 -0.49% to 23,913.70, Sensex -0.63% to 76,009.70**, as the crude rebound reversed the rupee's appreciation; FII outflows weighed, DII inflows cushioned, the Nifty MidCap printed a fresh high (+0.54%) — the structural-bear/tactical-relief split intact. By May 27: Nifty ~23,907 flat, Sensex ~75,868; **USD/INR back to ₹95.68 (+0.4%, rupee WEAKER)** — gave back the May 25 firming. **Structural bear intact and DOMINANT**: record FII outflow ₹1.92 lakh crore YTD 2026, FPI ownership ~16% (15-yr low, below DII), RBI has burned >$38B of reserves since Feb 28 (from $728.49B peak), rupee printed an all-time low ₹96.96 on May 20 before RBI off-market sales clawed it back. **The three deployment conditions — Brent <$95 sustained, FII turn positive, INR retrace to ₹93 — are still NOT jointly met** (operative Brent ~$99 near the threshold, but FII and INR moved the WRONG way). India only takes the bounce on a SIGNED deal, not a 'largely negotiated' one punctuated by strikes. (NOTE: operative Brent ~$99, NOT the yfinance -6.8%/$92.78 roll artifact.)

recent_signals.headline: **INDIA DUAL NARRATIVE MAY 25 — FOURTH FUEL HIKE BREAKS THE SATURDAY-ONLY PATTERN WHILE EQUITIES CATCH A DEAL-RELIEF RALLY (Nifty +1.32%, USD/INR ₹95.22 firmer).** In a clean-room domestic-only session (India open while US/UK closed for holiday), two opposite forces ran simultaneously. (1) **Fuel cascade ACCELERATED**: OMCs (IOC/BPCL/HPCL) raised petrol+diesel by ₹2.61-2.71/litre Monday May 25 — the FOURTH hike in under two weeks and the LARGEST single hike of the sequence (May 15 +₹3; May 19 +~90p; May 23 +~87-91p; May 25 +₹2.61-2.71), cumulative ~₹7.5/L since May 15; Mumbai petrol ₹111.21 / diesel ₹97.83, Kolkata petrol ₹113.51 (India TV News, Goodreturns May 25). **A large Monday hike on a US/UK holiday, the day before the RBI swap auction and the Quad, breaks the 'Saturday-only political-tolerance' thesis** — pass-through now tracks cost-recovery / refining-margin pressure + a weak rupee, not a political cadence. The April 27 fuel-pass-through trigger stays `active_fired` and is firing HARDER. (2) **Relief rally**: Nifty 50 +1.32% (+312.40) to 24,031.70; Sensex +1.42% (+1,073.61) to 76,488.96; Bank Nifty +2.3% (HDFC/ICICI/Axis led); USD/INR firmed to ₹95.22 (-1.0%, rupee STRONGER); India VIX -7.9% to ~16.5; India 10Y eased to 7.03% (-6 bps) — on US-Iran de-escalation hopes + Brent <$100 (oil import-bill/CAD/inflation relief) + RBI commentary the INR is 'undervalued' (first explicit RBI verbal-intervention note of the cycle) ahead of the $5B 3Y USD/INR swap auction Tuesday May 26. **Markets traded the geopolitics, not the domestic fuel squeeze.** (3) **But it is a crisis-relief bounce, NOT a structural re-rating** — FII flows, cumulative ₹7.5/L fuel pass-through, and RBI reserve drawdown are unchanged; deal-failure downside case is -4-6% (test 22,500-23,000), far larger than today's +1.3%, with ~75% of the gain reversing on deal-failure + oil re-spike. The Brent-sub-100 → India RELIEF leg fired cleanly (oil down → India up) for the first time empirically this cycle. Deployment posture stays cash-heavy pending Brent <$95 sustained + FII positive + organic USD/INR ₹93-94.
sources: India TV News May 25 (₹2.61-2.71 hike, cumulative ₹7.5, Mumbai/Kolkata city prices)
Business Standard May 25 market close (Nifty/Sensex levels, drivers, RBI 'INR undervalued' commentary)
Intel dossier 2026-05-25 A10, A11; Markets dossier A, B, C2

recent_signals.headline: **INDIA RAISED PETROL/DIESEL BY ~90 PAISE — SECOND HIKE IN A WEEK; APRIL 27 FUEL-PASS-THROUGH TRIGGER FIRES INSIDE 30-DAY WINDOW.** Petrol and diesel prices hiked again on May 19 across different cities (WION primary, Goodreturns city-wise increases 0.82-0.96 rupees). The second hike in a week confirms pass-through has been activated as a policy choice. **The mechanism**: India imports ~85% of crude; sustained Brent $109-111 + USD/INR ₹96 + the May 1 weekly-reserves drawdown of $7.79B forced OMCs to either continue absorbing the import-bill shock (IOC/BPCL/HPCL Q4 margin destruction, 3 weeks of stock-price punishment of OMCs has been pricing this) or pass it through to consumers (politically explosive, margin-protective). **The government chose to break the political seal** — pre-2024-election it deferred price hikes for 5 quarters; choosing TWO hikes in a week now signals fiscal patience is exhausted. **Follow-on**: 90 paise increase = ~0.85% inflation contribution if sustained; food/transport inflation will follow with 2-3 week lag. **The Sensex/Nifty +0.4%/+0.3% relief rally is misdirection** — Indian indices are catching the postponement-relief in IT exporter names (TCS, Infosys, HCL up 3-4.5% on weaker rupee benefit) while the underlying economy worsens. Classic 'good news for indices, bad news for the underlying economy' Indian-market schizophrenia in a stagflation tape. **Cleanest publicly-stated signal that India's deployment-condition #1 (Brent sustained <$95) is moving the WRONG direction — not just the asset price but the cascade through fiscal/inflation choices**. Deployment posture stays cash-heavy.
sources: Intel dossier 2026-05-19 A3, A4; Markets dossier C6
recent_signals.verification: India fuel hike ~90 paise = CONFIRMED ACTION (WION + Goodreturns multi-source); second hike in a week = CONFIRMED (week-over-week pricing data); Sensex/Nifty intraday relief = REPORTED (TradingEconomics intraday narrative single-source); IT-exporter rupee-weakness benefit = CONFIRMED analytical mechanism.

recent_signals.headline: **INDIAN MARKETS BOUNCED 2 CONSECUTIVE SESSIONS** — Sensex May 13 close 74,608.98 (+49.74 / +0.07%) snapping 4-session losing streak; Nifty 23,412.60 (+33.05 / +0.14%). May 14 morning extended: Sensex intraday high 75,071 (+462 / +0.61%), Nifty +170 to 23,581 (+0.72%); by 12:15 IST close prints: Sensex 75,389.46 (+1.0%), Nifty 23,679.85 (+1.1%), Bank Nifty 54,189.40 (+1.4%). Pharma + banking + metals + chemicals + cement leading; Cipla / Adani Enterprises / Bharti Airtel top gainers. **Bounce is summit-pause-pricing on Trump-Xi opening + HEU-via-China custodian door, NOT regime reversal — cumulative drawdown from May 11 still ~-1.83%.** USD/INR May 13 close ₹95.31 (4th straight session fresh closing record), May 14 morning ₹95.74; RBI intervention sustaining defence at ₹95.50-75 zone; pace MATERIALLY SLOWED from May 11-12 collapse. ₹96 trigger at Day 2 of 5-7 — not breached. RBI weekly reserves print due May 16 — last week was -$7.79bn; if this week >$10bn intervention sustainability moves from theoretical to operational concern. **A11 IRAN FM ARAGHCHI MET MODI AT BRICS-FM TABLE MAY 14 IN DELHI — SAME DAY TRUMP IN BEIJING, ONE DAY BEFORE MODI FLIES TO UAE** (CONFIRMED — Iran Embassy + Indian press multi-source). Joint call-on with all BRICS visiting FMs at 1pm IST from Sewa Teerth (Modi's new official complex). **MAJOR NEW SIGNAL: ARAGHCHI USED DELHI PLATFORM TO DIRECTLY THREATEN UAE** — 'Iranian FM threatens UAE, claims Tehran knew of Netanyahu's visit to Abu Dhabi' (Times of Israel May 14 liveblog primary CONFIRMED RHETORIC). India therefore receiving Iranian FM publicly threatening India's other Gulf strategic partner from Indian soil — extraordinary diplomatic optics. Indian press not editorialised (Republic/Times Now/Zee continue UAE-tour-as-energy-security framing without engaging Araghchi's UAE threat); MEA has not commented publicly. India strategic-autonomy-as-multi-vector-channel framing operationally tested in next 48 hours. **Modi 5-nation tour begins May 15 (UAE first leg).** Hardeep Puri ₹1,000 cr/day OMC pain quantification carries forward; no fresh government quantification. RBI economists note India can deploy ~$150bn before import cover falls to 2013 levels — meaningful buffer. Indian markets explicitly Trump-Xi-summit-pricing per Business Standard live blog (NDTV, post-Adani, ran Modi 5-nation tour with energy-security framing and ZERO engagement of UAE's combatant status). Brent retraced to $105.68 May 14 from $108.05 May 13 — single-session $108-110 trigger zone breach was NOT sustained; relief for India's import bill marginal but real. FII data via NSDL not yet released for May 13 settlement.

recent_signals.headline: DAY 2 CASCADE EXTENSION: Sensex closed May 12 at 74,559.24 (-1,456 pts, -1.92%) = LARGEST 2-SESSION DRAWDOWN OF CYCLE; cumulative ~16 LAKH CRORE m-cap erosion across 4 sessions; Nifty 50 closed 23,379.55 (-436.3 pts, -1.83%), BROKE BELOW 23,500 SUPPORT that had held since May 5-6 stabilisation; decline broad — IT, realty, consumer durables led down (IT sold on AI-pricing-pressure overhang). **USD/INR FRESH CLOSING RECORD ₹95.63 (vs ₹95.54 May 12 intraday in yesterday's brief); ₹95.50 RBI direct-intervention threshold NOW BROKEN IN CLOSE PRINTS, NOT JUST INTRADAY.** FIIs continued net sellers; multiple outlets confirm continuation rather than reversal. **MOODY'S CUT INDIA FY26 GROWTH 0.8PP TO 6.0% (from 6.8%)** citing subdued private consumption, capital formation, industrial activity amid higher energy costs — SOVEREIGN-RATING ACTOR (materially harder than yesterday's brokerage 6.7%→6.2% flag). May 13 intraday opened with relief bid (Sensex +700 pts from low, Nifty above 23,450 per BS LIVE; close prints in markets dossier: Nifty 23,496.80 +0.5%, Sensex 74,813.40 +0.3%, Bank Nifty 53,974.00 +0.8%) BUT this is intraday recovery from May 12 collapse, NOT regime reversal — cumulative drawdown still -1.83% from May 11 peak. **Union Minister HARDEEP PURI QUANTIFIED OMC PAIN FOR FIRST TIME: PSU oil companies losing ₹1,000 CRORE PER DAY; total under-recovery on track for ₹1.98 LAKH CRORE.** India has REINTRODUCED COVID-ERA WORK-FROM-HOME RULES as fuel-conservation measure (Fortune primary; Republic/Times Now/Zee continued domestic-politics framing as 'Modi's swadeshi push' rather than crisis-cause framing). **IRAN FM ARAGHCHI ARRIVES NEW DELHI MAY 13 FOR BRICS FM MEETING (May 14-15); MEETS MODI MAY 14 — same day Trump in Beijing, day before Modi flies to UAE.** India holds 2026 BRICS presidency. Agenda 'regional stability, multilateral cooperation, economic resilience' = code for India offering Iran non-aligned channel that is NOT Trump-led process. Most overt Indian strategic-autonomy choreography of cycle. **B5 PM MODI 5-NATION TOUR MAY 15-20 CONFIRMED — UAE (Sheikh Mohamed bin Zayed Al Nahyan, explicit 'energy security amid US-Iran war' framing + Comprehensive Strategic Partnership), Netherlands, Sweden, Norway, Italy (energy + emerging tech + defence).** UAE leg materially complicated by A10: Newsweek + WSJ now also report Saudi Arabia conducted secret strikes on Iran late March (REPORTED) — Modi will land in UAE (confirmed covert combatant) AND visit Iran's covert-combatant Gulf rivals in same week the Iranian FM is at Delhi BRICS table. No Indian outlet has editorialised against Modi visiting UAE knowing UAE struck Iran — diaspora-base in UAE (~3.5M Indians) makes anti-UAE framing politically unworkable. **India 10Y G-Sec 6.96-7.00% est. flat** (Moody's cut absorbed without further bond spike). Reversibility asymmetric: requires Brent <$95 + FII positive + INR ₹93 — none imminent. Sonal Varma (Nomura) call validated TWICE — ₹95.54 May 12 + ₹95.63 May 12 close; ₹96 trigger sits within 5-7 sessions per graph.

FII SINGLE-DAY NET SELL ₹8,437.56 cr MAY 11 (highest since April 24); DII +₹5,939.65 cr absorbed ~70%; YTD CY26 FII outflow ₹2.06 LAKH CR — exceeds full-year 2025 ₹1.66 lakh cr by ₹40k cr. MEA 'deeply concerned' statement urging restraint + 'unimpeded' Hormuz trade. **PM MODI 5-NATION TOUR MAY 15-20: UAE, Netherlands, Sweden, Norway, Italy** — energy/defence/trade diversification (UAE leg complicated by WSJ Lavan revelation). **FY27 GDP DOWNGRADE TO 6.2% FROM 6.7%** (global brokerage; 'historically large energy shock'). Sectoral: PSU OMCs (BPCL -2.34%, HPCL -2.65%, IOC -2.45% May 11) face Q1 FY27 losses large enough to wipe full-year FY26 profits per brokerage flags; Reliance May 12 intraday +1.99% (refining margin tailwind); Nifty IT -3% May 12 intraday (US-growth fears overwhelm rupee tailwind); Nifty Pharma -3% YTD vs Nifty -12% YTD (defensive bid intact). **STRUCTURAL STORY: US S&P 500 fresh record + NASDAQ +14.7% 1M on AI; KOSPI +33% 1M on AI semis; India takes whole brunt — worst import-FII-currency triple-vulnerability of major economies.**
